name: Addison Elementary School
description: Addison Elementary School is a place to play pickleball in Addison, NY. There are 2 outdoor asphalt courts. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. The courts cannot be reserved. The courts are free.

name: Bath
description: These pickleball courts are next to the new dog park in Bath. On Haverling Street just past Allen St. on the left.

name: BRAC
description: BRAC is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Painted Post, NY. There are 3 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The courts are private. Amenities include restrooms.

name: Corning Family YMCA
description: Corning Family YMCA is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Corning, NY. There are 2 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The facility includes amenities like restrooms, water, and lights. Reservations are available. A membership is required to play.

name: CPP Middle School Hawks
description: CPP Middle School Hawks is a place to play pickleball in Painted Post, NY. There are 4 outdoor asphalt courts. You’ll have to create your own lines and bring your own net. Players have access to restrooms. The courts are free.

name: Erwin Valley Elementary Tennis/Pickleball Courts
description:
Rebounding Wall also available (surface isn't ideal and bounces can be unpredictable)

Perimiter fence around courts can be annoying with a fence gap and an escaping balls hazard in places.

2 traditional tennis courts are also present but pickleball lines aren't present, but can be added DIY if needed. Nets have adjustable center strap for proper pickleball height adjustment.

name: Hammondsport Central High School
description: 2 courts are located on the tennis courts behind the school. Large parking area.

name: Hornell Area Family YMCA
description: Come play pickleball at Hornell Area Family YMCA in Hornell, NY! There are 2 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. Players have access to restrooms, water, and lights. You can reserve the courts. A membership is required to play.

name: Hornell Senior High School
description: Hornell Senior High School is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Hornell, NY. There are 5 outdoor hard courts. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. The facility includes amenities like lights. The courts are free.

name: Maple City Park
description: These are not dedicated pickleball courts, but are situated within the larger boundaries of tennis courts. The courts are new as of the summer of 2024.

name: Nasser Civic Center Rink
description: Nasser Civic Center Rink is a place to play pickleball in Corning, NY. There are 6 outdoor hard and concrete courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The courts cannot be reserved. Amenities include restrooms, water, and lights. The courts are free.
hours: M-F 8:30 am - 4:00 pm Payments Accepted: M-F 8:30 am to 3:30 pm Area Hiking Trails Canfield and Denison Park Study Ballfield Reservation Form Pavilion Request Form Parks Amenities Park Remediation Notice DEC Soil Contamination Information Official Website of the City of Corning, New York 500 Civic Center Plaza Corning, NY 14830 (607) 962-0340 Government Websites by Catalis | Privacy Policy

name: Prattsburgh Central School Ag Center
description: Pickleball court lines are painted on the tennis court farthest from the parking lot. Court is fenced in.

name: Pulteney Courts
description: These are outdoor courts painted on a tennis court. There are 2 portable nets secured to the fence that can be easily set up for pickleball use. Great view of Keuka Lake and convenient parking.

name: The Y at Watson Woods
description: The Watson Event Center hosts 3 indoor pickleball courts. The space is heated in the winter and can be converted into Air-nasium in the summer. There is parking in the rear of the building and across the street at our main office. We can loan equipment, Franklin X40s and misc paddles.
fees: Members only — 1-hr Court Reservations: $18 for YMCA members, $30 for non-members. 3 hour limit.

name: Veteran's Memorial Park
description: Veteran's Memorial Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Hornell, NY. There are 6 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free. Amenities include lights.

name: Woodhull Park
description: Woodhull Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Woodhull, NY. There are 2 outdoor asphalt courts. A tennis net is available, but you’ll need to create your own lines. Reservations are not available. The courts are free.
